headline: Iran deal lifts markets, oil back above $70
detail: A U.S.-Iran agreement to halt attacks is reducing geopolitical risk premium, giving stocks a Monday pop. For your tech and chip names, calmer macro means less headwind, but this is a sentiment lift, not a earnings driver — don't let it talk you out of necessary cuts in PLTR and AVGO.

headline: Trump Iran strikes historically spark Monday rallies
detail: Data shows these geopolitical-resolution rallies tend to be short-lived and fade during the week. That matters for a margin book — a one-day bounce in your losers doesn't mean the trend reversed, so don't use today's green as a reason to hold PLTR or AVGO past your stops.

headline: AeroVironment surges 19% on earnings, $1.2B backlog
detail: Strong defense tech earnings signal that government-facing tech spending remains healthy. This is indirect color for names like PLTR, which has a big defense/government revenue line — but PLTR's chart problem is its own issue regardless of sector tailwinds.
